title: Rule-and-Weather Regime Expert With Crossover Logic
summary: FIA rules and Pirelli tyre behavior imply qualitatively different stop logic in dry mandatory-compound races versus intermediate/full-wet crossover conditions.
rationale: FIA rules and Pirelli tyre behavior imply qualitatively different stop logic in dry mandatory-compound races versus intermediate/full-wet crossover conditions. Pirelli explicitly notes wide crossover windows for intermediates, while FIA dry-race tyre obligations create structural pressure that a single global model may smooth away.
implementation_hint: Train a small specialist only for wet/crossover rows (`INTERMEDIATE`/`WET`) and another for dry rows with explicit rule-state features: whether the car has likely satisfied the two-dry-compound obligation, whether current compound can finish, and whether a fresh alternative can finish. Blend specialists by regime or gate them hard by compound/weather state.
expected_effect: Could unlock gains on rare but strategically distinct subpopulations that a single global learner underfits, while also reducing variance from forcing one formula across dry and wet logic.
risk: Wet rows may be sparse, so the wet expert can overfit; inferring compound-obligation state from available columns may be imperfect if historical rule details vary across seasons.
